div1.entryFree: Long, vb. to belong (q.v.): no ceremony that to great ones --s, Meas. II, 2, 59. such grace as --eth to a lover's blessed case, Shr. IV, 2, 45. such austerity as --eth to a father, IV, 4, 7. it is an honour --ing to our house, All's IV, 2, 42. the child-bed privilege, which --s to women, Wint. III, 2, 104. the glories that l. to him, H5 II, 4, 80. to maintain the many to them --ing, H8 I, 2, 32. there --ed no more to the crown, II, 3, 48. to his surname --s more pride than pity to our prayers, Cor. V, 3, 170.
